mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of RSAFS versus forward 20-day returns remains below 0.03, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of months with rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, which depresses equity prices over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, the median 5-day forward S&P 500 return is greater than 0, or the signal yields less than 60% negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A high real interest rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) indicates restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate on future corporate cash flows, thereby lowering fair equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of real rate proxy remains insignificant (t-stat < 1.96) over rolling 3-year windows, or long-short portfolio based on real rate deciles fails to produce positive Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion when industrial capacity is stretched, further increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal overheating and rising input costs, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, which depresses equity prices over the medium term.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with high capacity utilization
failure_condition: In periods identified as late-cycle (INDPRO above historical 75th percentile and FEDFUNDS above 3%), median 20-day forward returns following INDPRO increases are positive rather than negative, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,500 thousand units signals a weakening real estate sector, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1500, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the signal fails to generate negative returns in more than 60% of instances within 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive equity returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Without labor market improvement, the interaction signal produces no average positive return, or the joint indicator has a Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects increasing residential investment and economic confidence, which positively impacts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days as a leading indicator.
failure_condition: If the 12-month correlation between HOUST changes and forward 20-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.1, or the signal produces positive returns less than 55% of the time when HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 5.0%, elevated short-term borrowing costs compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, the median 20-day forward return is positive, or the signal correctly predicts negative direction less than 55% of the time in out-of-sample testing.
